Part 36 (3) It is to be presumed (unless an earlier date for practical completion can be established) that practical completion of residential building work occurred on the earliest of whichever of the following dates can be established for the work—
(a) the date on which the contractor handed over possession of the work to the owner,
(b) the date on which the contractor last attended the site to carry out work (other than work to remedy any defect that does not affect practical completion),
(c) the date of issue of an occupation certificate under the Environmental Planning and Assessment Act 1979 that authorises commencement of the use or occupation of the work,
(d) (in the case of owner-builder work) the date that is 18 months after the issue of the owner-builder permit for the work.
In our view, in accordance with s 3B(1) of the HB Act, the statutory warranty period commenced on completion of the building works within the meaning of the contract; that is, when the works reached practical completion. If the contract had not provided for when the building works were complete and s 3B(1) was not engaged, then s 3B(2) and s 3B(3) of the HB Act would apply. It is not in dispute that the owners took possession of the works on 13 July 2017. In this case, that would be the relevant date in accordance with s 3B(3)(a) of the Act. Issue of the FOC is not the completion date of the works under s 3B.
Overall, we conclude that the Tribunal erred in finding that the date of completion of the building works was 20 October 2010, when the FOC was issued. In making this finding, the Tribunal misinterpreted the contract and did not correctly apply sections 48K(7) and 3B of the HB Act. Ground 6 is established. In our view, if the completion date was not the date of practical completion (8 July 2010), it was the date on which the works were handed over the owners (13 July 2021).
If we have erred in finding that the Tribunal’s finding in relation to completion date raises a question of law, then for the reasons set out above, we conclude that the Tribunal’s finding in this regard is against the weight of evidence. We would grant leave to appeal on this ground because we consider that the Tribunal’s finding concerning the completion date gives rise to an injustice to the builder which is reasonably clear.
Disposition of the appeal
It follows from our conclusion that the parties did not enter into a new contract or vary their original contract that the respondent’s application to the Tribunal was out of time. This is because the owners’ proceedings were not commenced within seven years of completion of the building works.
The Tribunal has no power to extend time for the making of an application under the HB Act: S & G Homes Pty Ltd t/as Pavilion Homes v Owen [2015] NSWCATAP 190 at [53].
In such circumstances, the appropriate orders are that the appeal be allowed, the decision under appeal be set aside and the Tribunal proceedings be dismissed.
Costs of the Tribunal proceedings

Finding a trustworthy dog walker can be a daunting task! Don't worry, I've got you covered. Here are some recommendations and services that can connect you with a reliable dog walker in your area:

**Online Platforms:**

1. **Rover**: Rover is one of the most popular platforms that connects dog owners with local dog walkers and sitters. You can browse profiles, read reviews, and book a walker that fits your needs.
2. **Care.com**: While primarily a childcare platform, Care.com also offers pet care services, including dog walking. You can search for walkers in your area and view their profiles.
3. **Wag!**: Wag! is a dog walking and dog sitting platform that connects you with local walkers. You can book a 30-minute or 1-hour walk, and even track your dog's route in real-time.
4. ** Barkly Pets**: Barkly Pets is a platform that connects dog owners with local dog walkers and sitters. You can browse profiles, read reviews, and book a walker that fits your needs.

**National Dog Walking Services:**

1. **Pet Sitters International**: Pet Sitters International is a network of professional pet sitters and dog walkers across the US. You can search for a walker in your area and contact them directly.
2. **National Association of Professional Pet Sitters**: The NAPPS is a professional organization that connects pet owners with local pet sitters and dog walkers. You can search for a walker in your area and view their profiles.

**Local Referrals:**

1. **Ask Friends, Family, or Neighbors**: Ask people you trust if they know of any reliable dog walkers in your area.
2. **Local Pet Stores or Veterinarians**: Visit local pet stores or veterinarians and ask if they know of any dog walkers they can recommend.
3. **Community Facebook Groups**: Join local Facebook groups or online forums and ask for dog walker recommendations.

**Tips for Finding a Reliable Dog Walker:**

1. **Check Reviews**: Always read reviews from multiple sources to get a sense of the walker's reliability and quality of service.
2. **Conduct an Interview**: Before booking a walker, conduct an interview to discuss your dog's needs, the walker's experience, and their approach to dog walking.
3. **Verify Insurance and Certifications**: Make sure the walker has liability insurance and any relevant certifications (e.g., pet first aid).
4. **Get a Meet-and-Greet**: Meet the walker in person with your dog to ensure they're a good fit.

Remember to always prioritize your dog's safety and well-being when searching for a dog walker. Good luck!

Determining the value of a piece of art is a complex process that involves several factors. While there's no one-size-fits-all formula, here are some key considerations that art appraisers, collectors, and dealers use to estimate the value of a piece:

**1. Artist's Reputation and Provenance:**
The artist's reputation, fame, and sales history play a significant role in determining the value of their work. The reputation of the artist, their exhibition history, and the prestige of the galleries or museums that have shown their work can all impact value.

**2. Rarity and Uniqueness:**
The scarcity of a piece, its uniqueness, or its place within the artist's oeuvre can increase its value. Limited edition prints, for example, can be more valuable than open-edition prints.

**3. Condition and State of Preservation:**
The condition of the artwork, including its state of preservation, restoration, or conservation, affects its value. Works in good condition, with minimal damage or restoration, are generally more valuable.

**4. Materials and Craftsmanship:**
The quality and rarity of materials, as well as the level of craftsmanship, can impact value. For example, a painting on high-quality canvas or a sculpture made from rare materials may be more valuable than one made with lower-quality materials.

**5. Market Demand:**
Current market trends, demand, and the availability of similar works can influence value. If there's high demand for a particular style or period, prices may increase.

**6. Size and Scale:**
The size of the artwork can impact its value, with larger works often commanding higher prices.

**7. Signature, Provenance, and Documentation:**
The presence of the artist's signature, documentation of ownership, and provenance (the record of ownership and exhibition history) can increase the value of a piece.

**8. Exhibition History and Critical Acclaim:**
The artwork's exhibition history, critical acclaim, and reviews can impact its value. Works exhibited in prominent museums or galleries, or those that have received critical acclaim, may be more valuable.

**9. Authentication and Certification:**
Verifying the authenticity of the artwork through expert opinion, certificates of authenticity, or scientific testing can increase its value.

**10. Comparative Sales:**
The value of similar artworks sold at auction or through private sales can provide a basis for estimating the value of a piece.

Keep in mind that these factors can interact with each other in complex ways, and the value of a piece of art is ultimately determined by what a buyer is willing to pay for it.

In the case of your flea market find, it's possible that the piece may not have all of these factors in its favor, but that doesn't mean it's not valuable or significant. Even if it's not a highly valuable work, it can still bring joy and appreciation to your daily life!

6. **BookNerd**: A fun app that helps you track your reading speed, comprehension, and retention. It also offers personalized reading challenges and recommendations.

Setting app-specific goals and scheduling app usage are powerful features in Moment that can help you develop healthier phone habits. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to set a daily time limit for social media in Moment:

**Setting a daily time limit for social media in Moment:**

1. **Open Moment:** Launch the Moment app on your device.
2. **Tap on "Apps":** Tap on the "Apps" tab at the bottom of the screen.
3. **Select "Social Media":** Tap on the "Social Media" category or select individual social media apps like Facebook, Instagram, or Twitter.
4. **Tap on "Set Goal":** Tap on the "Set Goal" button next to the social media category or individual app.
5. **Choose a daily time limit:** Select the daily time limit you want to set for social media. You can choose from preset options like 30 minutes, 1 hour, or 2 hours, or set a custom time limit.
6. **Schedule the goal:** Choose the days of the week and the time of day when you want the goal to be active. For example, you might set a goal to limit social media usage to 30 minutes per day, Monday to Friday, from 8am to 10pm.
7. **Save the goal:** Tap "Save" to save your goal.

**How Moment enforces the goal:**

Once you've set the goal, Moment will track your social media usage and alert you when you've reached your daily time limit. Here's how it works:

1. **Tracking:** Moment will track your social media usage in the background, monitoring how much time you spend in social media apps.
2. **Alerts:** When you've reached your daily time limit, Moment will send you a notification reminding you to take a break from social media.
3. **Blocking:** If you've enabled blocking, Moment will temporarily block access to social media apps until the next day or until you've reached a specific time of day when you've allowed yourself to use social media again.

**Tips and variations:**

* **Set multiple goals:** You can set multiple goals for different apps or categories, like setting a separate goal for email or games.
* **Customize your goals:** Experiment with different time limits and schedules to find what works best for you.
* **Use "Free Mode":** If you need to access social media for work or other important reasons, you can enable "Free Mode" to temporarily disable the goal.
* **Review and adjust:** Regularly review your goal progress and adjust your goals as needed to ensure you're meeting your phone habits goals.

By setting app-specific goals and scheduling app usage in Moment, you'll be able to develop healthier phone habits, reduce distractions, and make more intentional choices about how you spend your time.

Lemon tetras and zebra danios are both excellent choices for a 20-gallon community tank. Here's some information about their behavior and tank requirements to help you create a thriving environment for them:

**Lemon Tetras (Hyphessobrycon citrinus)**

* **Schooling behavior:** Lemon tetras are a schooling fish and should be kept in a group of at least 10-15 individuals. They're very active and love to swim together, creating a mesmerizing display of shimmering yellow scales.
* **Tank requirements:**
	+ Water temperature: 72-82°F (22-28°C)
	+ pH: 6.0-7.0
	+ Water hardness: Soft to moderately hard
	+ Water flow: Moderate to strong
	+ Tank size: Minimum 10 gallons, but 20 gallons or larger recommended for a school
* **Diet:** Omnivorous, feed on small invertebrates, algae, and plant matter in the wild. In captivity, provide a varied diet of high-quality commercial flakes, pellets, and frozen or live foods like brine shrimp or bloodworms.
* **Behavior:** Peaceful, active, and curious. They can be skittish and may take time to settle in a new tank.

**Zebra Danios (Danio rerio)**

* **Schooling behavior:** Zebra danios are also schooling fish and should be kept in a group of at least 6-10 individuals. They're very active and love to swim together, creating a striking display of horizontal stripes.
* **Tank requirements:**
	+ Water temperature: 65-75°F (18-24°C)
	+ pH: 6.5-8.0
	+ Water hardness: Soft to hard
	+ Water flow: Moderate to strong
	+ Tank size: Minimum 10 gallons, but 20 gallons or larger recommended for a school
* **Diet:** Omnivorous, feed on small invertebrates, algae, and plant matter in the wild. In captivity, provide a varied diet of high-quality commercial flakes, pellets, and frozen or live foods like brine shrimp or bloodworms.
* **Behavior:** Active, hardy, and tolerant of changes in water conditions. They're also very curious and love to explore their surroundings.

**General tips for keeping schooling fish:**

* Provide plenty of open swimming space for your school to roam and exercise.
* Avoid keeping fin-nippers or aggressive fish that may harass your schooling fish.
* Include plenty of hiding places and visual barriers, such as plants or decorations, to help your school feel secure.
* Regularly monitor water parameters and perform regular water changes to maintain a healthy environment.
* Feed your school multiple times a day, as they have high metabolisms and need frequent meals.

Both lemon tetras and zebra danios are excellent choices for a 20-gallon community tank, and they can be kept together in a harmonious environment. Just make sure to research and understand their specific needs and requirements before introducing them to your tank.

4. Wake up earlier: Consider waking up 30 minutes earlier each day to fit in a quick workout before your day begins. This can help energize you and set a positive tone for the day ahead.

5. Use your lunch break: Instead of eating a full lunch or sitting at your desk, use your lunch break to go for a walk or do a short workout. Even 10-15 minutes of activity can be beneficial.

6. Make use of technology: There are many fitness apps, workout videos, and online classes available that you can do at home or on-the-go, making it easier to fit workouts into your busy day.

Remember, even small amounts of exercise can have a significant impact on your health, so don't feel like you need to do a full-hour workout every day. The important thing is to move your body regularly and make physical activity a consistent part of your routine.

Pricing your herbal teas and infused oils can be a bit tricky, but I've got some tips to help you get started:

**Research the Market:**

1. **Competitor Analysis**: Research your competitors, both online and offline, to determine their pricing strategies.
2. **Market Trends**: Stay up-to-date on market trends, seasonal demand, and consumer behavior to adjust your pricing accordingly.
3. **Target Audience**: Consider your target audience's willingness to pay and their expectations from your products.

**Cost-Based Pricing:**

1. **Calculate Your Costs**: Calculate the cost of ingredients, packaging, labeling, and labor to determine your minimum selling price.
2. **Markup**: Add a markup to your costs to determine your selling price. A common markup range is 2-5 times the cost.

**Value-Based Pricing:**

1. **Unique Selling Points**: Identify your products' unique selling points, such as organic, handcrafted, or locally sourced, and price accordingly.
2. **Quality and Potency**: Consider the quality and potency of your herbal teas and infused oils, and price them higher if they offer superior benefits.
3. **Brand Image**: If you're building a premium brand, you may be able to charge a higher price based on your brand's reputation and customer loyalty.

**Pricing Strategies:**

1. **Tiered Pricing**: Offer different tiers of products with varying prices, such as a budget-friendly option and a premium option.
2. **Bundle Deals**: Offer discounts for bulk purchases or bundle deals to incentivize customers to buy more.
3. **Seasonal Pricing**: Adjust your pricing seasonally to reflect changes in demand and ingredient availability.

**Pricing Examples:**

1. **Herbal Teas**: $5-15 per ounce, depending on the quality and rarity of the herbs.
2. **Infused Oils**: $10-30 per ounce, depending on the quality of the oil and the herbs used.

**Tips for Pricing Your Products:**

1. **Start Low**: Start with a lower price point and adjust as needed based on customer feedback and market demand.
2. **Be Transparent**: Clearly communicate the value and benefits of your products to justify your pricing.
3. **Be Flexible**: Be prepared to adjust your pricing strategy as your business grows and evolves.

**Resources:**

1. **The Herbal Academy's Pricing Guide**: A comprehensive guide to pricing herbal products, including teas and infused oils.
2. **The Small Business Administration's Pricing Guide**: A general guide to pricing strategies for small businesses.

Remember, pricing is an iterative process, and you may need to adjust your prices as you gather more data and customer feedback. Stay flexible, and don't be afraid to experiment and try new pricing strategies!

Congratulations on your writing progress and participation in the writing challenge! Organizing your files can help you track your progress, easily locate specific pieces, and even inspire new ideas. Here are some suggestions for categorizing and storing your writing files:

**Categorization Ideas:**

1. **Genre**: Create folders or labels for different genres, such as Short Stories, Poetry, Flash Fiction, Non-Fiction, etc.
2. **Project-based**: Organize files by specific projects, like individual short stories or poetry collections. This can help you keep all related files and drafts together.
3. **Theme or Topic**: Categorize files by theme, like "Memory and Nostalgia" (perfect for your "The Smell of Old Books" piece!), "Fantasy", "Romance", etc.
4. **Chronological**: Organize files by date, with folders or labels for each year, month, or quarter. This can help you track your progress over time.
5. **Writing Challenge**: Create a folder or label specifically for your writing challenge submissions, with subfolders for each prompt (e.g., "Forgotten Memories", "Future Prompts", etc.).

**Storage Options:**

1. **Digital Folders**: Create a hierarchical folder structure on your computer or cloud storage service (e.g., Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive). This allows easy access and organization of your files.
2. **Cloud-based Note-taking Apps**: Consider using apps like Evernote, Simplenote, or Bear to store and organize your writing files. These apps often have tagging and searching features to help you quickly find specific pieces.
3. **Physical Binders or Folders**: If you prefer a more tactile approach, use physical binders or folders to store printed copies of your work. This can be helpful for reviewing and editing your work offline.
4. **Hybrid Approach**: Combine digital and physical storage by keeping digital copies of your files and printing out select pieces for a physical archive.

**Additional Tips:**

1. **Use clear and descriptive file names**: Include the title, date, and any relevant keywords in your file names to make them easy to find.
2. **Keep multiple drafts and versions**: Store different versions of your work to track changes and progress.
3. **Add metadata or tags**: Use keywords, tags, or metadata to add context to your files, making them easier to search and categorize.
4. **Regularly back up your files**: Ensure your files are safe by regularly backing them up to an external hard drive, cloud storage, or both.

I hope these suggestions help you organize your writing files and keep track of your progress!

2. **Assign a Home:** Designate a specific spot in your utensil holder for each group of utensils. This will help you develop a habit of putting things back in their place.
3. **Use Dividers:** Add dividers or small compartments within your utensil holder to separate groups of utensils. This will prevent clutter and make it easier to find what you need.
4. **Store Frequently Used Items Front and Center:** Place your most frequently used utensils in easy-to-reach locations, such as the front or top of your utensil holder.
5. **Utilize Vertical Space:** If you have a tall utensil holder, use the vertical space by storing longer items like spatulas, whisks, or wooden spoons.
6. **Store Small Items in Small Containers:** Use small containers or baskets within your utensil holder to store small items like garlic presses, lemon squeezers, or pastry brushes.
7. **Hang Utensils:** Consider using hooks or a utensil holder with a hanging feature to store items like pots, pans, or utensils with holes in the handles.
8. **Label Your Utensil Holder:** Label each section or compartment in your utensil holder to help you and others quickly identify where items belong.

**Utensil Holder Ideas:**

1. **Mason Jar Utensil Holder:** Use a mason jar or a similar container with a wide mouth to store utensils. You can add a decorative ribbon or label to give it a personal touch.
2. **Tiered Utensil Holder:** Invest in a tiered utensil holder that allows you to store utensils in separate levels. This is perfect for maximizing vertical space.
3. **Drawer Dividers:** If you have a kitchen drawer dedicated to utensils, use dividers or inserts to separate groups of utensils and keep them organized.
4. **Wall-Mounted Utensil Holder:** Consider a wall-mounted utensil holder that can hold a variety of utensils, freeing up counter space.
5. **Rotating Utensil Holder:** Use a rotating utensil holder that allows you to easily access utensils and prevents clutter from building up.

**Additional Tips:**

1. **Clean Your Utensil Holder Regularly:** Remove any food residue or debris from your utensil holder to prevent bacteria growth and keep your kitchen clean.
2. **Store Utensils by Task:** Organize your utensils by the tasks they're used for, such as baking, cooking, or serving. This will help you quickly find the right tool for the job.

By implementing these tips, you'll be able to maximize the space in your utensil holder, reduce clutter, and make your kitchen a more efficient and enjoyable space to cook and create!

Turbinado sugar is a great choice! Its partially refined state and subtle caramel flavor make it an excellent addition to many recipes. Pairing it with other ingredients can indeed enhance its flavor and create complex taste profiles. Here are some suggestions for ingredients that complement turbinado sugar well:

1. **Spices:** Turbinado sugar and warm spices like cinnamon, nutmeg, or cardamom create a delightful combination. Try pairing it with these spices in baked goods, such as sweet breads or cookies.
2. **Citrus:** The brightness of citrus fruits like oranges, lemons, or limes balances the richness of turbinado sugar. Use it in citrus-infused desserts, like lemon bars or orange creamsicles.
3. **Nuts:** Turbinado sugar and nuts like almonds, pecans, or hazelnuts make a great team. Add them to baked goods, like cakes or cookies, or use them as a topping for ice cream or yogurt.
4. **Espresso or coffee:** The deep, bold flavor of espresso or coffee pairs beautifully with turbinado sugar. Try using it in mocha-flavored desserts, like brownies or ice cream.
5. **Vanilla:** The creamy, sweet flavor of vanilla complements turbinado sugar nicely. Use it in desserts like crème brûlée, ice cream, or cakes.
6. **Cocoa powder or dark chocolate:** Turbinado sugar and cocoa powder or dark chocolate create a rich, indulgent flavor profile. Use them in brownies, cakes, or truffles.
7. **Dried fruits:** Cranberries, cherries, or apricots add natural sweetness and chewiness to baked goods, while turbinado sugar provides a deeper flavor.

Getting a great sound out of open D tuning on an electric guitar like the Les Paul requires some adjustments to your playing technique, amp settings, and guitar setup. Here are some tips to help you get the best sound out of open D tuning on your Les Paul:

**Guitar Setup:**

1. **String choice:** Use a set of strings with a heavier gauge (0.012 - 0.052 or 0.013 - 0.056) to take advantage of the open D tuning's lower pitch. Heavier strings will provide a fuller, more resonant tone.
2. **Action and intonation:** Adjust the action (string height) and intonation to ensure the guitar plays smoothly and in tune, especially in the lower registers.
3. **Pickup height:** Lower the pickup height to reduce the output and prevent the guitar from sounding too bright or harsh.

**Amp Settings:**

1. **Gain:** Reduce the gain to around 5-6 to prevent the amp from overdriving and creating a muddy sound.
2. **Bass:** Boost the bass around 100-150 Hz to add warmth and weight to the sound.
3. **Midrange:** Cut the midrange frequencies (500-800 Hz) to reduce the guitar's natural brightness and create a more balanced tone.
4. **Treble:** Reduce the treble frequencies (5k-8k Hz) to prevent the sound from becoming too harsh or brittle.

**Playing Technique:**

1. **Right-hand technique:** Use a more relaxed, gentle right-hand technique to produce a smooth, even tone. Avoid using a heavy pick or aggressive strumming.
2. **Left-hand technique:** Focus on playing with a more subtle, nuanced left-hand technique, using finger slides and vibrato to add expression to your playing.
3. **String selection:** Experiment with playing on different strings to find the ones that produce the best tone. The lower strings (D, A, E) tend to sound fuller and more resonant in open D tuning.

**Additional Tips:**

1. **Use the neck pickup:** The neck pickup tends to produce a warmer, more mellow tone that's well-suited for open D tuning.
2. **Experiment with different rhythms:** Open D tuning lends itself well to more laid-back, rhythmic playing styles. Experiment with different rhythms and grooves to find what works best for you.
3. **Add some compression:** Using a compressor pedal or plugin can help even out your dynamics and sustain, creating a more consistent tone.
4. **Try different amp models:** Experiment with different amp models or profiles to find one that complements the tone of your Les Paul in open D tuning.

By following these tips, you should be able to get a great sound out of open D tuning on your Les Paul. Remember to experiment and find what works best for your playing style and musical preferences.

A 2-bay and 4-bay NAS device refer to the number of hard drive bays available on the device. Here's a breakdown of the key differences:

**2-Bay NAS:**

* Has two hard drive bays, allowing you to install two hard drives or solid-state drives (SSDs)
* Typically more compact and affordable than 4-bay NAS devices
* Suitable for small to medium-sized networks with limited storage needs
* Often used for:
	+ Small businesses or home offices with limited data storage requirements
	+ Personal cloud storage for a single user or small family
	+ Media streaming and sharing for a small number of devices

**4-Bay NAS:**

* Has four hard drive bays, allowing you to install four hard drives or SSDs
* Generally larger and more expensive than 2-bay NAS devices
* Suitable for larger networks or those with higher storage demands
* Often used for:
	+ Medium to large businesses with multiple users and departments
	+ Home networks with many devices and large media collections
	+ Centralized storage for multiple users or teams

To determine which one is right for you, consider the following factors:

1. **Storage capacity:** Calculate how much storage you need based on your files, videos, and other data. A 2-bay NAS can provide up to 16TB of storage (2 x 8TB drives), while a 4-bay NAS can offer up to 32TB (4 x 8TB drives) or more.
2. **Number of users:** If you have a small number of users (1-5), a 2-bay NAS might be sufficient. For larger groups or teams, a 4-bay NAS can provide more storage and better performance.
3. **Data redundancy:** If you want to set up a RAID (Redundant Array of Independent Disks) configuration for data redundancy and protection, a 4-bay NAS is often a better choice. This allows you to configure RAID 5 or 6, which provides better data protection and performance.
4. **Future expansion:** If you anticipate your storage needs will grow in the future, a 4-bay NAS can provide more flexibility and upgrade options.
5. **Budget:** 2-bay NAS devices are generally more affordable than 4-bay models. If budget is a concern, a 2-bay NAS might be a better starting point.
6. **Space and design:** If you have limited space or prefer a more compact design, a 2-bay NAS might be a better fit.

Ask yourself these questions to help determine which type of NAS is right for you:

* How much storage do I need today, and how much might I need in the future?
* How many users will be accessing the NAS, and what are their storage needs?
* Do I need data redundancy and protection, and if so, what type of RAID configuration do I require?
* What is my budget for the NAS device and hard drives?
* Do I have space constraints or design preferences that might influence my decision?

By considering these factors, you can choose the right NAS device for your needs, whether it's a 2-bay or 4-bay model.

Arranging plants in a raised bed can be a fun puzzle! To maximize space and sunlight, consider the following tips for your tomato, pepper, cucumber, and marigold combination:

**Sunlight and Orientation:**

* **South-facing**: Since your raised bed will receive the most direct sunlight from the south, place the plants that require the most sun (tomatoes and peppers) on the south side.
* **East-west rows**: Plant in east-west rows to minimize shading between plants and ensure each row receives direct sunlight.

**Plant Spacing and Arrangement:**

* **Tomatoes**:
	+ Plant indeterminate tomatoes (vining varieties) along the north side of the bed, using a trellis or cage for support. This will allow them to grow upwards and outwards, without shading other plants.
	+ Plant determinate tomatoes (bush varieties) in the center or south side of the bed, leaving about 18-24 inches (45-60 cm) between plants.
* **Peppers**:
	+ Plant peppers near the tomatoes, but slightly offset to ensure good air circulation. Leave about 12-18 inches (30-45 cm) between pepper plants.
* **Cucumbers**:
	+ Train cucumbers to climb up a trellis or other support on the north or east side of the bed. This will keep them off the ground, reduce soil-borne diseases, and make harvesting easier.
	+ Plant cucumbers about 12-18 inches (30-45 cm) apart.
* **Marigolds**:
	+ Plant marigolds around the perimeter of the bed, about 6-12 inches (15-30 cm) apart. They'll help deter nematodes and other pests, and add a pop of color to your garden.

**Companion Planting:**

* **Tomatoes and peppers**: Planting these together can improve their growth and flavor, as they have similar growing requirements.
* **Cucumbers and marigolds**: Marigolds can help repel nematodes, which can harm cucumbers.
* **Tomatoes and cucumbers**: While they can be planted together, make sure to provide enough space and support for the cucumbers to climb, as they can spread out and shade tomatoes.

**Additional Tips:**

* **Air circulation**: Leave about 1-2 feet (30-60 cm) between rows to ensure good air circulation and prevent disease spread.
* **Soil depth**: Make sure your raised bed has a minimum depth of 6-8 inches (15-20 cm) to accommodate the roots of your plants.
* **Watering**: Water your plants deeply and regularly, but avoid overwatering, which can lead to disease and root rot.

By following these guidelines, you'll be able to create a thriving and space-efficient garden in your raised bed, with all your plants receiving the sunlight and care they need. Happy gardening!

Meal prep is a game-changer, isn't it? It's amazing how much time and stress it can save during the week. I'd be happy to help you with some new lunch recipe ideas that are perfect for meal prep!

Here are some delicious and easy-to-make options:

**International Inspirations**

1. **Korean-Style BBQ Beef Bowls**: Marinate beef in a Korean-inspired sauce, cook with veggies, and serve over rice.
2. **Indian-Style Lentil Curry**: Cook lentils and add to a flavorful curry sauce with veggies and serve over rice or with naan bread.
3. **Mexican Chicken Fajitas**: Sauté chicken and veggies with fajita seasoning, serve with tortillas, avocado, and salsa.

**Salads and Wraps**

1. **Quinoa Salad Jars**: Layer quinoa, roasted veggies, and a protein of your choice (e.g., chicken, tofu, or chickpeas) in a jar.
2. **Turkey and Avocado Wraps**: Spread hummus on a tortilla, add sliced turkey, avocado, lettuce, and tomato.
3. **Grilled Chicken and Strawberry Spinach Salad**: Top a bed of spinach with grilled chicken, sliced strawberries, crumbled feta, and a balsamic vinaigrette.

**Global Soups**

1. **Thai Coconut Soup**: Simmer coconut milk, chicken or tofu, and veggies in a flavorful broth.
2. **Lentil and Vegetable Stew**: Cook lentils and add to a hearty stew with veggies and your choice of protein (e.g., sausage or chickpeas).
3. **Roasted Vegetable Soup**: Blend roasted veggies with chicken or vegetable broth and cream for a creamy soup.

**Pasta and Rice Bowls**

1. **Pesto Chicken Pasta**: Toss cooked pasta with pesto, grilled chicken, and cherry tomatoes.
2. **Chicken and Veggie Fried Rice**: Cook rice, chicken, and veggies in a wok or large skillet with soy sauce and serve with a fried egg.
3. **Cajun Shrimp and Rice Bowl**: Cook shrimp in a spicy Cajun seasoning, serve with rice, steamed veggies, and a side of crusty bread.

These ideas should give you a great starting point for your meal prep. You can always adjust the ingredients and spices to suit your taste preferences. Happy cooking and meal prepping!

To do this, you'll need to know the total cost of the 50-pound batch of feed. Once you have that information, you can easily calculate the cost per pound.

Here's the formula:

Cost per pound = Total cost of feed ÷ Total weight of feed (in pounds)

For example, let's say the total cost of the 50-pound batch of feed is $25.00.

Cost per pound = $25.00 ÷ 50 pounds = $0.50 per pound

So, in this example, the cost per pound of the layer feed would be $0.50.

1. **Promote your Live session in advance**: Create buzz around your upcoming Live session by promoting it on your social media channels, email newsletters, and website. Share behind-the-scenes content, sneak peeks, or teasers to generate interest.
2. **Choose a catchy title and description**: Craft an attention-grabbing title and description for your Live session. Make sure they're informative, concise, and include relevant keywords.

4. **Use engaging thumbnails**: Create eye-catching, high-quality thumbnails that showcase the main topic of your Live session. This will help grab users' attention and encourage them to click on your video.
5. **Interact with your audience**: Encourage viewers to participate by asking questions, hosting Q&A sessions, or asking for feedback. Respond to comments and engage with your audience in real-time to build a sense of community.
6. **Offer exclusive content**: Provide unique content or offers during your Live session to incentivize viewers to tune in. This could be a free resource, a discount, or a sneak peek into upcoming content.
7. **Collaborate with influencers or guests**: Partner with influencers, experts, or guests in your niche to bring new perspectives and attract their followers to your Live session.
8. **Use Facebook's built-in features**: Leverage Facebook's built-in features, such as polls, quizzes, and reactions, to increase engagement and encourage audience participation.
9. **Repurpose your Live content**: After the Live session, repurpose the content into other formats like blog posts, Instagram Stories, or YouTube videos to reach a broader audience.
10. **Analyze and optimize**: Use Facebook Insights to track your Live session's performance, and analyze the data to identify areas for improvement. Adjust your strategy based on your findings to optimize future Live sessions.
11. **Provide value**: Focus on providing valuable, informative, or entertaining content that resonates with your audience. This will encourage viewers to share your Live session with others and increase engagement.
12. **Be consistent**: Regularly schedule Live sessions to maintain a consistent presence and keep your audience engaged. This will help build anticipation and expectations around your Live content.

To increase views specifically, consider the following:

* **Pin your Live session to the top of your Facebook page** to make it more visible to your audience.
* **Use Facebook's "Watch Party" feature** to allow users to co-watch and discuss your Live session with friends.
* **Share your Live session in Facebook Groups** related to your niche to reach a targeted audience.
* **Add a call-to-action (CTA) in your Live session** to encourage viewers to share the video with others or visit your website.

By implementing these strategies, you can increase engagement, views, and overall success on your Facebook Live sessions.

Setting a budget for skincare and beauty products can help you prioritize your spending and make more intentional purchasing decisions. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you set a realistic budget and find affordable alternatives:

**Step 1: Assess Your Current Spending**

* Review your past purchases and track your spending on skincare and beauty products for the past 3-6 months.
* Identify your average monthly spend and categorize your purchases (e.g., skincare, makeup, haircare, etc.).

**Step 2: Determine Your Budget**

* Based on your assessment, set a realistic monthly budget for skincare and beauty products. Consider your financial goals, income, and expenses.
* Allocate a specific amount for each category (e.g., skincare, makeup, etc.).

**Step 3: Identify Your Must-Haves**

* Make a list of the products you use regularly and can't live without.
* Prioritize these products and allocate a larger portion of your budget to them.

**Step 4: Find Affordable Alternatives**

* Research affordable alternatives for your must-have products.
* Look for:
	+ Dupes: Similar products from different brands at a lower price point.
	+ Drugstore or pharmacy brands: Often offer quality products at a lower price than high-end brands.
	+ Online retailers: Websites like Amazon, Ulta, or Sephora often offer discounts, sales, or loyalty programs.
	+ Samples: Try samples before committing to a full-size product.

**Step 5: Plan Your Purchases**

* Create a shopping list based on your budget and must-haves.
* Set a schedule for purchasing products (e.g., every 2-3 months).
* Avoid impulse buys by sticking to your list and schedule.

**Affordable Skincare Alternatives:**

* Cetaphil Moisturizing Cream (similar to La Mer Moisturizing Cream)
* Paula's Choice 2% BHA Liquid (similar to Dr. Dennis Gross Skincare Alpha Beta Peel)
* Olay Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Cream (similar to high-end anti-aging moisturizers)

**Affordable Beauty Alternatives:**

* Maybelline Fit Me! Foundation (similar to high-end foundation brands)
* NYX Professional Makeup HD Finishing Powder (similar to high-end setting powders)
* L'Oreal Paris Voluminous Lash Paradise Mascara (similar to high-end mascara brands)

Remember, setting a budget and finding affordable alternatives is not about sacrificing quality, but about being intentional with your spending and making smart choices. By following these steps, you'll be able to enjoy your favorite products while staying within your budget.

**Average Finish Time for a Half-Marathon:**

The average finish time for a half-marathon varies depending on several factors, such as the course terrain, weather conditions, and the runner's experience level. However, based on various sources, here are some general guidelines:

* For beginners, the average finish time is around 2:30-3:00 hours.
* For intermediate runners, the average finish time is around 2:00-2:20 hours.
* For advanced runners, the average finish time is around 1:45-2:00 hours.

**Tips for Improving Your Half-Marathon Time:**

Considering your personal best is 2:15 hours, here are some tips to help you shave off some minutes:

1. **Incorporate interval training:** Incorporate interval workouts into your training schedule, such as alternating between fast and slow paced runs. This will help improve your running efficiency and endurance.
2. **Increase your weekly mileage:** Gradually increase your weekly mileage to build up your endurance. Aim to increase your weekly mileage by 10-15% each week.
3. **Incorporate strength training:** Incorporate strength training exercises 2-3 times a week to target your core, glutes, and legs. This will help improve your running efficiency and reduce your risk of injury.
4. **Focus on proper form and technique:** Focus on proper running form and technique, such as maintaining good posture, landing midfoot or forefoot, and keeping your arms relaxed. This will help you run more efficiently and reduce your risk of injury.
5. **Incorporate hill repeats:** Incorporate hill repeats into your training schedule to improve your running efficiency and build strength in your legs.
6. **Practice fueling and hydration:** Practice your fueling and hydration strategy during your long runs to ensure you're properly fueled and hydrated on race day.
7. **Taper before the race:** Taper your training 2-3 weeks before the race to allow your body to rest and recover.
8. **Get enough rest and recovery:** Make sure to get enough rest and recovery time between runs. Aim for 7-9 hours of sleep each night and take rest days as needed.
9. **Mental preparation:** Work on your mental preparation by visualizing your success, setting realistic goals, and staying positive.

**Specific Training Plan:**

Considering your current fitness level and goal, I would recommend the following training plan:

* Weeks 1-4: Base building phase - Focus on building your weekly mileage and incorporating strength training exercises.
* Weeks 5-8: Build phase - Incorporate interval training and hill repeats into your schedule.
* Weeks 9-12: Taper phase - Gradually reduce your training volume and intensity to allow your body to rest and recover.

Remember to listen to your body and adjust your training plan as needed. It's also essential to consult with a running coach or a medical professional to create a personalized training plan.

You're in the right city! New York City is a treasure trove for ancient culture enthusiasts. Besides the Metropolitan Museum of Art, there are several other museums and exhibits that are must-visits. Here are some recommendations:

1. **American Museum of Natural History (AMNH)**: While not exclusively focused on ancient cultures, the AMNH has an impressive collection of artifacts and exhibits on ancient civilizations, including the Hall of Human Origins, the Hall of Saurischian Dinosaurs (with a focus on ancient environments), and the Rose Center for Earth and Space.
2. **Brooklyn Museum**: The Brooklyn Museum has a significant collection of ancient artifacts, including Egyptian, Greek, and Roman art and artifacts. Their exhibit on "Egypt Reborn: Art for Eternity" is a highlight.
3. **New York University Institute for the Study of the Ancient World (ISAW)**: This museum is dedicated to the study of the ancient world, with a focus on the Mediterranean and Near East. They have a rotating exhibit schedule, but their permanent collection includes artifacts from ancient Mesopotamia, Greece, and Rome.
4. **The Morgan Library & Museum**: While primarily a library and museum focused on rare books and manuscripts, the Morgan has an impressive collection of ancient and medieval artifacts, including illuminated manuscripts and papyri.
5. **El Museo del Barrio**: This museum is dedicated to Latin American and Caribbean art and culture, but they also have a collection of pre-Columbian artifacts from ancient Mesoamerica and South America.
6. **The Hispanic Society of America**: This museum is dedicated to Spanish, Portuguese, and Latin American art and culture, but they also have a collection of ancient artifacts from the Iberian Peninsula and the Americas.

Some special exhibits and events to keep an eye out for:

* **The Metropolitan Museum of Art's Temple of Dendur**: This ancient Egyptian temple, built around 15 BCE, is located in the Met's Sackler Wing. It's a must-see for anyone interested in ancient Egyptian architecture and culture.
* **The Brooklyn Museum's antiquities collection**: The Brooklyn Museum has a rotating exhibit schedule, but their antiquities collection is always worth a visit. They have an impressive collection of Greek, Roman, and Egyptian artifacts.
* **The New York University Institute for the Study of the Ancient World's (ISAW) lecture series**: ISAW regularly hosts lectures and events on various topics related to the ancient world. These events are usually free and open to the public.

These are just a few of the many museums and exhibits in New York City that are worth exploring. As an ancient culture enthusiast, you'll find plenty to keep you engaged and inspired in the city that never sleeps!

Fresh herbs can elevate any salad to a whole new level!

Congratulations on starting to water your herb garden every morning! Consistent watering is essential for healthy herb growth. Here are some tips to help you keep your fresh herbs fresh for a longer period:

1. **Store them properly**: After harvesting, gently rinse the herbs with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Pat them dry with a clean towel or paper towels to remove excess moisture. Store them in a sealed container or plastic bag in the refrigerator to maintain humidity and keep them fresh.
2. **Keep them cool**: Most herbs prefer cooler temperatures, so store them in the refrigerator at a temperature between 32°F and 40°F (0°C and 4°C).
3. **Monitor moisture**: Make sure the herbs aren't too wet or too dry. Check on them daily and remove any wilted or brown leaves to prevent spoilage.
4. **Trim and prune**: Regular trimming and pruning encourage bushy growth and prevent the herbs from flowering. This will also help you use up the older leaves and promote new growth.
5. **Use the right storage container**: Use a container with good air circulation to prevent moisture buildup. You can also use a herb keeper or a Mason jar with a paper towel to absorb excess moisture.
6. **Freeze them**: If you have an abundance of herbs, consider freezing them to preserve their flavor and aroma. Simply chop the herbs, place them in an airtight container or ice cube tray, and store them in the freezer.
7. **Harvest in the morning**: Herbs are typically at their best in the morning, after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day. This is when they're most flavorful and aromatic.
8. **Use the right herbs for the job**: Different herbs have different shelf lives. For example, basil and cilantro are more delicate and have a shorter shelf life, while rosemary and thyme are heartier and can last longer.

Some popular herbs that pair well with salads include:

* Basil: Sweet and aromatic, perfect for caprese salads or as a topping for fruit salads.
* Cilantro: Fresh and citrusy, great for Mexican-inspired salads or as a garnish for soups.
* Parsley: Mild and fresh, excellent for Mediterranean-style salads or as a garnish for soups and sandwiches.
* Dill: Bright and citrusy, pairs well with fish or as a topping for yogurt-based salads.
* Mint: Cooling and refreshing, perfect for fruit salads or as a garnish for desserts.

Enjoy your salad and happy gardening!

What a crucial topic! Film festivals have been at the forefront of promoting diversity and inclusion in the industry, providing a platform for underrepresented voices and stories to be heard. Here's how festivals are working to increase representation and opportunities for underrepresented groups:

**Increasing Representation:**

1. **Diverse Programming:** Festivals actively curate diverse lineups, showcasing films that highlight underrepresented communities, including women, people of color, LGBTQ+, and people with disabilities.
2. **Inclusive Juries:** Festivals ensure that juries and selection committees comprise diverse individuals, reducing unconscious bias and promoting a more inclusive selection process.
3. **Targeted Initiatives:** Many festivals launch specific initiatives, such as women's film programs, LGBTQ+ showcases, or diversity-focused competitions, to amplify underrepresented voices.

**Creating Opportunities:**

1. **Mentorship Programs:** Festivals offer mentorship programs, pairing emerging filmmakers from underrepresented groups with industry professionals, providing guidance and support.
2. **Workshops and Panels:** Festivals host workshops, panels, and masterclasses, focusing on topics like diversity, equity, and inclusion, to educate and empower underrepresented filmmakers.
3. **Networking Events:** Festivals organize networking events, allowing underrepresented filmmakers to connect with industry professionals, potential collaborators, and funders.

**Addressing Industry-Wide Issues:**

1. **Diversity and Inclusion Panels:** Festivals host panels and discussions addressing industry-wide issues, such as lack of representation, bias, and systemic barriers, to raise awareness and spark change.
2. **Industry Partnerships:** Festivals partner with industry organizations, guilds, and studios to promote diversity and inclusion initiatives, driving systemic change.
3. **Data Collection and Analysis:** Festivals collect and analyze data on diversity and inclusion, providing valuable insights to inform industry-wide initiatives.

**Challenges and Opportunities:**

1. **Systemic Change:** Festivals must address systemic barriers and biases within their own structures, ensuring that diversity and inclusion efforts are authentic and sustainable.
2. **Resource Constraints:** Smaller festivals may face resource constraints, limiting their ability to implement diversity and inclusion initiatives.
3. **Sustainability:** Festivals must ensure that diversity and inclusion efforts are sustainable, with long-term impact, rather than one-off initiatives.
4. **Intersectionality:** Festivals must consider intersectionality, acknowledging that individuals may identify with multiple underrepresented groups, and address these complexities in their programming and initiatives.

**Examples of Festivals Leading the Way:**

1. **Sundance Film Festival:** Sundance has been a pioneer in promoting diversity and inclusion, with initiatives like the Native American and Indigenous Film Program and the Sundance Institute's Women at Sundance program.
2. **Toronto International Film Festival (TIFF):** TIFF has introduced initiatives like the TIFF Talent Accelerator, supporting emerging filmmakers from underrepresented groups, and the TIFF Share Her Journey campaign, promoting women in film.
3. **Cannes Film Festival:** Cannes has launched the Cannes Film Festival's "Towards a More Inclusive Festival" initiative, aiming to increase diversity and inclusion in its programming and industry events.

By actively promoting diversity and inclusion, film festivals are driving positive change in the industry, providing opportunities for underrepresented voices to be heard, and creating a more inclusive and equitable film ecosystem.

Pricing your baked goods can be a delicate task, as you want to ensure you're making a profit while still being competitive and appealing to customers. Here are some tips to consider when pricing your baked goods for your next event:

1. **Calculate your costs**: Start by calculating the cost of ingredients, packaging, and any other expenses (like transportation, equipment, or market fees) associated with producing each item. This will give you a baseline price.
2. **Know your target audience**: Understand who your customers are and what they're willing to pay. If you're selling at a high-end event or in an affluent area, you may be able to charge more than at a smaller, local market.
3. **Research the competition**: Check what similar bakers are charging for similar products at the same event or in your area. You don't want to price yourself out of the market, but you also don't want to underprice your goods.
4. **Consider the portion size and quality**: If your baked goods are larger or of higher quality than others, you may be able to charge more. Conversely, smaller or more basic items may need to be priced lower.
5. **Think about the event itself**: If the event is a one-day festival or a special occasion, people may be more willing to pay a premium for treats. On the other hand, if it's a weekly market, you may need to be more competitive.
6. **Price in increments**: Instead of pricing each item individually, consider offering bundles or packages with discounts for larger purchases. This can encourage customers to buy more and increase your overall sales.
7. **Round numbers are attractive**: Pricing items at round numbers (e.g., $2, $5, $10) can make them more appealing to customers, as they're easier to calculate and make change for.
8. **Test and adjust**: Don't be afraid to experiment with different prices and adjust based on customer feedback and sales data.

For your next event, you could consider increasing your prices by 10-20% to test the waters. If you're unsure, you could also offer a "limited time introductory offer" or a "special promotion" to gauge customer reaction.

Some general guidelines for pricing baked goods are:

* Muffins: $2-$4 each
* Scones: $3-$5 each
* Cookies: $1-$3 each (or $5-$10 for a dozen)

Remember to stay flexible and be prepared to adjust your pricing strategy based on customer feedback and sales data. Good luck at your next event!

Sorting and filtering are essential skills to master in a spreadsheet, and I'm happy to guide you through it.

**Sorting:**

Sorting allows you to arrange your data in a specific order, such as alphabetical or chronological. To sort your loyalty program data:

1. Select the entire table by clicking on the top-left corner of the table or pressing Ctrl+A (Windows) or Command+A (Mac).
2. Go to the "Data" menu and click on "Sort & Filter" (in Google Sheets) or "Sort" (in Microsoft Excel).
3. Choose the column you want to sort by (e.g., "Expiration Date").
4. Select the sorting order (e.g., "Ascending" or "Descending").
5. Click "Sort" to apply the sorting.

For example, if you sort the "Expiration Date" column in ascending order, the programs with the nearest expiration dates will appear at the top of the list.

**Filtering:**

Filtering allows you to narrow down your data to show only specific information. To filter your loyalty program data:

1. Select the entire table by clicking on the top-left corner of the table or pressing Ctrl+A (Windows) or Command+A (Mac).
2. Go to the "Data" menu and click on "Sort & Filter" (in Google Sheets) or "Filter" (in Microsoft Excel).
3. Click on the filter icon (a small triangle) in the header row of the column you want to filter (e.g., "Expiration Date").
4. Select "Filter by condition" and choose the condition you want to apply (e.g., "Is before" or "Is within the next").
5. Enter the specific date or date range you want to filter by (e.g., "April 15th" or "Within the next 30 days").
6. Click "OK" to apply the filter.

For example, if you filter the "Expiration Date" column to show only programs expiring within the next 30 days, you'll see a list of programs that require your attention soon.

**Tips:**

* Use the "Filter" function to quickly see which programs have rewards expiring soon or to focus on a specific type of reward (e.g., points, miles, or cash back).
* Use the "Sort" function to prioritize your programs based on factors like expiration date, reward type, or balance.
* You can also use conditional formatting to highlight cells that meet certain conditions, such as rewards expiring soon or balances below a certain threshold.

Now that you know the basics of sorting and filtering, you can easily manage your loyalty programs and stay on top of your rewards.

The builder's obligations under the contract (irrespective of the definition and meaning of "building works", "date of practical completion" and "practical completion") were not complete at least until the owners received the FOC as part of planning and building approvals under item 11 in Sch 1; that provides the date that work is complete within the meaning of the original contract (a date is not required, just a specified activity) on the proper interpretation of the contract and also within the meaning of HBA s 3B(1). Accordingly, s 3B(2) and (3) have no work to do because s 3B(2) operates when s 3B(1) is not operative and s 3B(3) is parasitic on s 3B(2) because it requires a context of practical completion for the presumption about practical completion to operate.
Conclusion on grounds 6 and 7
In summary, the Tribunal’s reasoning process in relation to completion date was:
The builder was responsible for obtaining the FOC;
The builder’s obligation to provide the FOC fell within the definition of “building works” under the contract
Completion of the works occurred within the meaning of the contract and therefore for the purposes of s 3B(1) of the HB Act upon the owners’ receipt of the Final Occupation Certificate (FOC);
The Tribunal’s finding in this respect is relevant to its finding that there was consideration in respect of the new contract it found that the parties had entered into. Given our findings in respect of Grounds 2 and 3, it is not strictly necessary for us to consider this ground, but we do so for completeness.
We conclude that the Tribunal erred in its interpretation of relevant terms of the contract.
For this purpose we set out the relevant clauses of the contract, which is a standard form Housing Industry Association (HIA) contract.
Clause 1 of the contract defines “building works” as:
The building works to be carried out, completed and handed over to the owner in accordance with this contract as shown in the contract documents and including variations.
In accordance with clause 1, “practical completion” means:
When the building works are complete except for minor omissions and defects that do not prevent the building works from being reasonably capable of being used for their usual purpose.
Clause 7 of the contract is headed “Planning and Building Approvals”. Clause 7.1states:
7.1 The party named in Item 11 of Schedule 1 must obtain and pay for all building and planning approvals.
Item 11 names the builder as the responsible party for the purposes of clause 7.

I'm glad you asked! Staying organized and managing your time effectively is crucial when volunteering for multiple charity events. Here are some tips to help you stay on top of your commitments:

**1. Create a master calendar**: Keep a calendar specifically for your charity events and volunteer work. Write down all the events, dates, times, and deadlines. This will help you visualize your commitments and avoid conflicts.

**2. Prioritize your events**: Identify the most important events and focus on those first. Consider the impact, urgency, and deadlines for each event.

**3. Break down tasks into smaller chunks**: Divide larger tasks into smaller, manageable chunks. This will help you stay focused and avoid feeling overwhelmed.

**4. Set reminders and notifications**: Set reminders and notifications for upcoming events, deadlines, and tasks. You can use apps like Google Calendar, Trello, or Asana to stay organized.

**5. Designate a "charity day"**: Choose a specific day of the week or month to dedicate to charity work. This will help you stay focused and ensure that you're making time for your volunteer commitments.

**6. Learn to say "no"**: Be mindful of your capacity and learn to say "no" to events that may interfere with your existing commitments or personal life.

**7. Communicate with event organizers**: Keep event organizers informed about your availability, constraints, and any changes to your schedule.

**8. Take breaks and self-care**: Don't forget to take breaks and practice self-care. Volunteering can be physically and emotionally demanding, so make sure you're taking time to rest and recharge.

**9. Keep track of your hours**: Keep a record of your volunteer hours, mileage, and expenses. This can be helpful for tax purposes and also help you track your impact.

**10. Celebrate your achievements**: Acknowledge and celebrate your accomplishments, no matter how small they may seem. This will help you stay motivated and encouraged to continue making a difference.

By following these tips, you'll be able to stay organized, manage your time effectively, and make a meaningful impact in your community. Remember to stay flexible and adapt to changes as needed. Good luck, and keep making a difference!

Jazz is a wonderful genre! There's so much to explore, and I'd be happy to recommend some jazz musicians and albums to get you started. Here are a few:

**Classic Jazz Legends:**

1. **Miles Davis**: A legendary trumpeter and bandleader, known for his cool, introspective style. Must-listen albums: "Kind of Blue" and "Birth of the Cool".
2. **John Coltrane**: A saxophonist and composer who revolutionized jazz with his intense, spiritual playing. Must-listen albums: "A Love Supreme" and "Giant Steps".
3. **Charlie Parker**: A virtuosic saxophonist and composer known for his lightning-fast solos and harmonic innovations. Must-listen albums: "Bird: The Complete Charlie Parker on Verve" and "Charlie Parker with Strings".
4. **Thelonious Monk**: A pianist and composer known for his unique, percussive style and quirky compositions. Must-listen albums: "Monk's Dream" and "Straight, No Chaser".

**Modern Jazz Masters:**

1. **Kamasi Washington**: A saxophonist and composer who blends jazz, funk, and hip-hop. Must-listen albums: "The Epic" and "Heaven and Earth".
2. **Robert Glasper**: A pianist and producer who combines jazz, R&B, and hip-hop. Must-listen albums: "Black Radio" and "Everything's Beautiful".
3. **Cécile McLorin Salvant**: A vocalist known for her interpretive skills and eclectic repertoire. Must-listen albums: "The Window" and "Dreams and Daggers".
4. **Brad Mehldau**: A pianist and composer known for his introspective, lyrical style. Must-listen albums: "The Art of the Trio" and "Highway Rider".

**Essential Jazz Albums:**

1. **"Miles Smiles" by Miles Davis**: A 1967 album featuring Davis's second great quintet, with Herbie Hancock, Wayne Shorter, and Tony Williams.
2. **"A Love Supreme" by John Coltrane**: A 1965 album featuring Coltrane's classic quartet, with McCoy Tyner, Jimmy Garrison, and Elvin Jones.
3. **"Take Five" by The Dave Brubeck Quartet**: A 1959 album featuring Brubeck's iconic 5/4 time signature and Paul Desmond's saxophone.
4. **"Mingus Ah Um" by Charles Mingus**: A 1959 album featuring Mingus's unique bass playing and compositions.

**Online Resources:**

1. **Jazz at Lincoln Center**: A website featuring live performances, educational resources, and articles about jazz.
2. **JazzTimes**: A magazine and website covering jazz news, reviews, and interviews.
3. **All About Jazz**: A website featuring jazz news, reviews, and articles, as well as a comprehensive jazz database.

These are just a few examples to get you started. Jazz is a rich and diverse genre, and there's much more to explore. Enjoy your journey!

Now, about planting your tomatoes and other seedlings outside...

The ideal temperature for planting tomatoes and other warm-season crops outside depends on the specific variety, but generally, you want to wait until the soil has warmed up and the air temperature is consistently above a certain threshold.

For tomatoes, the ideal temperature for planting outside is when the soil temperature reaches around 55°F (13°C) to 60°F (16°C). This is usually when the air temperature is around 65°F (18°C) to 70°F (21°C) during the day.

However, if you're in a region with frost, you'll want to wait until the last frost date for your area has passed. You can check the average last frost date for your area online.

For other seedlings, like marigolds, the ideal temperature for planting outside varies. Marigolds are relatively hardy and can tolerate cooler temperatures, but they still prefer warmer weather. A good rule of thumb is to wait until the soil temperature reaches around 50°F (10°C) to 55°F (13°C) for marigolds.

Before planting, make sure to harden off your seedlings by gradually exposing them to outdoor conditions over the course of 7-10 days. This will help them adjust to sunlight, wind, and temperature fluctuations.

Remember to also check the specific instructions for each type of seedling or plant you're growing, as some may have different requirements.

Good luck with your planting, and I hope your marigolds continue to thrive!

I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot and provide cleaning and maintenance tips to keep your coffee maker running smoothly.

**Common Issues after 3 Weeks of Use:**

1. **Scale buildup:** Mineral deposits from water can cause issues with brewing performance.
2. **Old coffee oils:** Residual coffee oils can affect flavor and clog the machine.
3. **Dust and debris:** Accumulation of dust and debris can impact brewing performance.

**Cleaning and Maintenance Tips:**

1. **Descale your machine:** Every 3-6 months, mix equal parts water and white vinegar in the water reservoir. Run a brewing cycle to descale the machine. Repeat the process a few times to ensure all mineral deposits are removed.
2. **Run a cleaning cycle:** Check your user manual for a specific cleaning cycle or setting. If not available, run a cycle with a mixture of equal parts water and white vinegar.
3. **Clean the brew basket and grinds container:** Remove any built-up coffee oils and residue by washing the brew basket and grinds container with warm soapy water. Rinse thoroughly and dry.
4. **Wipe down the exterior:** Use a damp cloth to wipe down the machine's exterior, paying attention to any areas with visible buildup or stains.
5. **Check and clean the water reservoir:** Ensure the water reservoir is clean and free of any buildup. Run a mixture of equal parts water and white vinegar through the reservoir to remove any mineral deposits.
6. **Use filtered water:** If you live in an area with hard water, consider using a water filter or a water softener to reduce mineral buildup in your machine.
7. **Regularly clean the coffee maker's internal components:** Check your user manual for guidance on cleaning internal components, such as the showerhead or brew tube.
8. **Run a fresh water cycle:** After cleaning and descaling, run a fresh water cycle to rinse out any remaining vinegar or cleaning solution.

**Additional Tips:**

* Use a coffee maker cleaning solution or a product specifically designed for cleaning coffee makers.
* Avoid using abrasive cleaners or scouring pads, as they can damage the machine's surfaces.
* If you notice any mold or mildew, mix equal parts water and baking soda to create a paste. Apply the paste to the affected area and let it sit for 30 minutes before rinsing.

By following these cleaning and maintenance tips, you should be able to resolve any performance issues with your coffee maker. If problems persist, refer to your user manual or contact the manufacturer's customer support for assistance.

Congratulations on taking the eco-friendly route with Luna's potty training! Those training pads from Chewy.com are a great choice. Here are some additional potty-training aids and tips that might be helpful:

**Potty-Training Aids:**

1. **Potty Bells**: Teach Luna to ring a bell (like a dog doorbell) when she needs to go outside. This helps her communicate her needs and can speed up the training process.
2. **Potty Pads with Attractant**: Some potty pads, like those from PetSafe, have a built-in attractant that encourages puppies to use them. These might be a good alternative or addition to your current pads.
3. **Outdoor Potty Areas**: Designate a specific outdoor area, like a puppy pad or a grassy spot, where you want Luna to go potty. Make it easily accessible and clean up any accidents to avoid creating negative associations.
4. **Cleaning Products**: Invest in a good enzyme-based cleaner, like Nature's Miracle, to remove any accidents from your floors or carpets. These products can help eliminate lingering scents that might attract Luna to the same spot again.

**Tips:**

1. **Establish a Schedule**: Stick to a consistent feeding and potty schedule to help Luna learn when it's time to go. Take her outside immediately after meals, naps, and playtime.
2. **Supervise, Supervise, Supervise**: Keep an eye on Luna at all times, especially in the early stages of potty training. This will help you catch any accidents before they happen and reinforce good behavior.
3. **Praise and Reward**: Positive reinforcement is key! Praise Luna and reward her with treats, praise, and affection when she uses the potty pads or goes potty outside correctly.
4. **Watch for Signs**: Puppies will often sniff and circle before they go potty. If you see Luna exhibiting these behaviors, quickly take her to the designated potty area.
5. **Be Patient**: Accidents will happen, so don't get discouraged. It's all part of the learning process. Stay calm, clean up the mess, and try to prevent it from happening again in the future.
6. **Gradually Increase Freedom**: As Luna becomes more reliable with potty training, you can start giving her more freedom in the house, such as access to more rooms or longer periods of time without supervision.

Setting a goal for a certain number of short stories per month can be a great way to maintain momentum and challenge yourself to continue producing new work. This approach is often called a "writing challenge" or "writing streak." Here are some pros and cons to consider:

**Pros:**

1. **Consistency**: Setting a goal can help you establish a regular writing habit, which is essential for improving your craft and making progress on your projects.
2. **Productivity**: Aiming for a specific number of short stories per month can motivate you to be more productive and focused during your writing sessions.
3. **Variety**: Writing multiple short stories in a short period can help you experiment with different genres, styles, and themes, which can be beneficial for your growth as a writer.
4. **Portfolio building**: The more you write, the more content you'll have to share with others, whether it's through submissions, self-publishing, or sharing with writing groups.

**Cons:**

1. **Quality over quantity**: Focusing too much on quantity might lead to sacrificing quality. Make sure you're not rushing through stories just to meet a numerical goal.
2. **Burnout**: Writing multiple short stories in a short period can be exhausting, especially if you're new to writing or have other commitments. Be careful not to overwork yourself.
3. **Unrealistic expectations**: Setting an unrealistic goal can lead to disappointment and frustration if you're unable to meet it. Be honest about your schedule, writing speed, and energy levels.

To make a goal like this work for you, consider the following:

1. **Start with a manageable goal**: Based on your current writing pace and schedule, set a realistic goal for the number of short stories you can complete in a month. For example, if you've written four stories so far, you might aim for six or eight in the next month.
2. **Focus on completion, not perfection**: Remember that your first draft doesn't need to be perfect. Focus on completing the story, and you can always revise and edit later.
3. **Leave room for flexibility**: Life can be unpredictable, and unexpected events may affect your writing schedule. Don't be too hard on yourself if you miss a story or two; adjust your goal as needed.
4. **Celebrate milestones**: Acknowledge and celebrate your progress, even if you don't meet your exact goal. This will help you stay motivated and encouraged.

In your case, since you've written four short stories so far, you might consider setting a goal of six to eight stories for the next month. This will challenge you to maintain your momentum while still allowing for some flexibility. Remember to review and adjust your goal regularly to ensure it remains realistic and motivating for you.

3. **Experiment with different brands**: Try out different brands and flavor profiles to find the one that you enjoy the most.
4. **Make your own BBQ sauce**: If you're feeling adventurous, try making your own BBQ sauce from scratch. This will allow you to customize the flavor to your liking.

Yes, Instagram Insights is a fantastic tool to help you track your performance and understand what's working and what's not. Insights provides you with data on your account's performance, including:

1. **Account metrics**: Follower growth, reach, and engagement rates.
2. **Content metrics**: Performance of individual posts, including likes, comments, saves, and engagement rates.
3. **Audience metrics**: Demographics, interests, and behaviors of your followers.

To access Instagram Insights, make sure your account is set to a Business or Creator profile. Then, follow these steps:

1. Open the Instagram app and navigate to your profile page.
2. Tap the three horizontal lines ( Settings) on the top-right corner.
3. Tap "Insights" from the menu.

In Insights, you'll find several sections:

1. **Overview**: A summary of your account's performance over the past 7 days.
2. **Content**: A detailed view of your individual posts, including metrics like engagement rate, reach, and impressions.
3. **Audience**: Demographic information about your followers, including age, gender, location, and interests.
4. **Reach**: A breakdown of how many people saw your posts and how they interacted with them.

To get the most out of Insights, focus on the following metrics:

1. **Engagement rate**: The percentage of followers who engaged with your post (likes, comments, saves, etc.).
2. **Reach**: The number of unique users who saw your post.
3. **Save rate**: The percentage of users who saved your post.
4. **Top-performing content**: Identify the types of posts that perform well and create more content like that.

Now, to optimize your content for your 1250 followers, consider the following:

1. **Post types**: Analyze which types of posts (photos, videos, IGTV, Reels) perform better with your audience.
2. **Content themes**: Identify the topics or themes that resonate with your audience and create more content around those.
3. **Hashtags**: Use relevant hashtags that your audience is interested in to increase visibility.
4. **Timing**: Experiment with different posting times to see when your audience is most active.
5. **Captions and CTAs**: Craft engaging captions and use CTAs to encourage interaction.

By analyzing your Insights and adjusting your content strategy accordingly, you'll be able to:

* Increase engagement and reach
* Attract new followers
* Improve your content's overall performance

Remember, Insights is a powerful tool, but it's up to you to interpret the data and make informed decisions about your content strategy.

Apex Legends is an amazing game! Playing with friends is a huge part of its appeal. As for your question, it really depends on your group's goals and preferences. Here are some points to consider:

**Focusing on ranking up to level 150:**

Pros:

1. **Sense of accomplishment**: Reaching level 150 is a significant milestone, and you'll feel a sense of pride and accomplishment when you get there.
2. **Improved skills**: Grinding to level 150 requires consistency and dedication, which can help you improve your skills, learn the maps, and develop strategies.
3. **Better rewards**: As you level up, you'll unlock new legends, skins, and other cosmetic items, which can be a great motivator.

Cons:

1. **Pressure and stress**: Focusing too much on ranking up can lead to frustration, toxicity, and stress when you're not performing well or encountering setbacks.
2. **Limited flexibility**: If you're too focused on ranking up, you might feel obligated to play with a specific legend or strategy, which can limit your flexibility and experimentation.

**Playing for fun and not worrying about leveling up:**

Pros:

1. **Relaxed atmosphere**: Playing for fun allows you to enjoy the game without the pressure of ranking up, which can lead to a more relaxed and enjoyable experience.
2. **Experimentation and creativity**: Without the pressure of ranking up, you're free to try out new legends, strategies, and playstyles, which can be a great way to learn and improve.
3. **Social aspect**: Focusing on fun allows you to prioritize socializing with your friends, communicating, and working together as a team.

Cons:

1. **Less sense of progression**: Without a clear goal like ranking up, you might feel like you're not making progress or achieving anything significant.
2. **Less motivation**: If you're not working towards a specific goal, you might lose motivation or interest in the game.

Ultimately, it's up to you and your friends to decide how you want to approach your Apex Legends session. You could try a mix of both approaches: set a goal to reach a certain level or complete a specific challenge, but also make sure to take breaks and have fun along the way.

Here's a suggestion:

* Set a goal to play a certain number of matches or reach a specific level, but don't make it too rigid.
* Take breaks and rotate legends or playstyles to keep things fresh and exciting.
* Focus on communication and teamwork to improve your overall gameplay and have more fun.
* Don't be too hard on yourselves if you don't reach your goals; remember, the primary goal is to have fun with your friends!

Have a blast playing Apex Legends with your friends, and may the best squad win!
